frameworks.name: working memory load and oscillatory dynamics
frameworks.one_line: WM load modulates theta/alpha/beta spectral power across cortical sources

frameworks.name: training-induced neural efficiency
frameworks.one_line: modality-specific training reshapes load-related neural processing for efficient maintenance

frameworks.name: modality-specific vs modality-irrelevant transfer
frameworks.one_line: auditory training contrasted with tactile training and no-training controls

core_question: How does modality-specific auditory working memory training change load-related neural processing during high-demand WM tasks?
key_assumptions: post-training efficiency modulates neural processing under high load
EEG source-space spectral power indexes WM-relevant cortical activity
manipulations: WM load (low vs high) in auditory task
key_findings: modality-specific training improved behavioral WM performance versus control
load increased frontal theta and decreased parietal theta across groups
centro-parietal alpha and central beta decreased with higher load
auditory-trained group showed trend toward reduced right medial temporal beta at high load
interpretations: reduced beta in voice-selective regions suggests more efficient maintenance of task-relevant stimuli after modality-specific training
limitations_noted: right medial temporal beta effect reported as a tendency rather than confirmed effect
